title: Rollback ('R')
text: Rollback ('R') Roll back the transaction. Description Roll back the transaction on the current logon path. If the DBMS does not support Rollback, return 0 to Uniface. Connectors that are TM-controlled ( uctrl->TMEnabled = 1) will not receive any Commit or Rollback requests. From Uniface Preconditions realized by Uniface—The connector is logged on to the DBMS. Table 1. Information Available from Uniface Data Structure->Element Description uctrl->iobuf Points to the general I/O buffer. uctrl->sqlbuf Points to the SQL buffer. uctrl->uchfw Uniface virtual address of the next control block. uctrl->uchbw Uniface virtual address of the previous control block. uctrl->udrvini Unique identification of the current logon path. uctrl->udrvnum Connector sequence number. uctrl->ulock Lock indicator. To Uniface The Rollback request is provided with a dummy control block. This control block does not describe a table and it does not have an associated connector workspace. If the connector maintains the field uctrl->ulock and the Rollback has succeeded, uctrl->ulock must be set to 0 for every control block that meets all the following criteria: It is for the DBMS driven by this connector ( uctrl->udrvnum equals udrvnum in the first control block). It belongs to the current logon path ( uctrl->udrvini equals udrvini in the first control block). The corresponding table is open ( uctrl->uopen is greater than 0). Table 2. Connector Request Return Values Value Description 0 Commit was successful. -3 Exceptional error. -9 I/O server logon error. -16 DBMS reported network error. If the connector request return value is less than 0, an error code must be supplied in uctrl->udrverr . Service Functions Not applicable. Extra information See the extra information in Commit ('C'). TM-Controlled Connectors (Commit request) Connectors that are TM-controlled ( uctrl->TMEnabled=1 ) do not receive any Commit or Rollback requests. The transaction manager sends these requests directly to the DBMS. Therefore, TM-controlled DBMS connectors cannot perform resource management and internal administration when a transaction has ended. Also, TM-controlled DBMS connectors cannot administer uctrl->ulock . Related Links uctrl Exchanges general information between Uniface and the connector, and describes a table. Commit ('C') Commit the transaction on the current logon path. If the DBMS does not support Commit, return 0 to Uniface. TM-Controlled DBMS Connectors A database connector can be controlled by a transaction manager only if it is TM-capable. Uniface does not provide TM-controlled connectors and does not directly support third-party transaction managers. However, you can use the APIs provided by the Database Connector Interface to build a custom database connector that does. Was this topic helpful? Like Dislike
